title: quercus lanuginosa var. conglomerata
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. conglomerata, also known as the velvet oak, is a type of evergreen tree that belongs to the Fagaceae family. Traditionally, the bark of this tree has been used in herbal medicine, and it is valued for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and protect against infections. The health benefits of Quercus lanuginosa var. conglomerata are diverse, including the potential to aid in wound healing, lower blood pressure, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the bark of the velvet oak is often used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions.
